text: Matthew chapter 11, beginning with verse 25 and reading through verse 30. And the idea was that Mr. Christian need to find a way to get that burden off his back, lest it sink him down to the lowest hell. Our text is interesting because there is a, connection here between two important truths. One is the predestination of God, and the other is the responsibility of man. In verses 25 to 27, we see the doctrine of predestination is given a prominent emphasis here. Our Lord has declared the sovereignty of God when it comes to who is revealed the way of salvation. And we find that in verse 25, that the Father has hidden the truth of salvation from some. He has revealed it unto others. And it's because they did not have revealed unto them the way of salvation. They did not understand it when he preached it. They did not come to know the Lord. But it was all up to the Father. It pleased the Father to hide it from them and not reveal it to them. In verse 27, you'll notice that only of those who come to the Father and can know the Father and can know the Son are those to whom the Son reveals the Father. And so we see this sovereignty of God here. But it's also interesting that connected with this, in verses 28 to 30, we see the gospel call here. We see the message going out for people to come to Christ. for salvation. We must always balance these two out. We must always hold to both truths that God predestinates, and yet the message is for man to repent of his sins and come to Christ. And so we see the Christ here inviting or calling, I should say, those who are tottering under a heavy load and burden like Mr. Christian to come to him for relief and for salvation. But we should also recognize in this text of Scripture that we always, as Christians, need to be coming to Christ for relief from our burden and sin, for we fall into sin every day.
